title: Jessie Buckley's Golden Globes Speech For Hamnet Best Actress Win
text: Jessie Buckley won the Golden Globe for Best Actress for her role in Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao. This victory followed her earlier win at the Critics Choice Awards. Hamnet is a fictionalized exploration of William Shakespeare's life during the composition of Hamlet. The narrative centers on the playwright and his wife portrayed by Buckley as they navigate family tragedy, including the loss of their 11-year-old son. The film later earned the Golden Globe for Best Picture Drama. During her speech, Buckley emphasized the film's diverse crew. She stated: we were telling the story of probably the most famous Brit that ever lived and we had a Chinese director, a lot of Irish, a mostly Polish crew beside our British family. She expressed gratitude to director Zhao, co-star Paul Mescal, author Maggie O'Farrell, and other cast members including Emily Watson and Jacobi Jupe. Buckley humorously acknowledged Polish key grip Tomasz Sternicki for preparing stew on set. Buckley became the first Irish person to win in this acting category. She also commended her fellow nominees, calling Julia Roberts a hero to actresses everywhere.
